title: Strengthen template and adopter stability matrix
outOfScope: Trust Layer raw intent and multilingual detection reserved for Work Items 2 and 3.
Release publication and execution-plan cleanup reserved for Work Items 7 and 8.

sources.reason: Review requires template reliability and stable governance after project import.

riskAssessment.reason: Documentation and existing end-to-end evidence are bounded to template and adopter lifecycle behavior.

preReviewWarnings: Review template bootstrap, adopter upgrade, rollback, and compatibility evidence.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: docs/template-adopter-stability-matrix.md contains rows for template quality, compatibility, first adoption, governance journey, and lifecycle closure with evidence references.
The matrix references tests/test_adoption_e2e.py, tests/test_project_governance_journey.py, and .github/workflows/compatibility.yml.
The matrix explicitly limits claims to declared matrix entries and minimal adopter fixtures.
guidelines: Document evidence boundaries and residual adopter risk.

intent.problem: The repository has lifecycle checks but their template-versus-adopter coverage is not presented as one auditable stability matrix.
constraints: Use existing executable tests as evidence.
Do not claim every external adopter stack is validated.
intent.rationale: A matrix makes reliability claims reviewable and exposes which lifecycle stages are actually exercised.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Template quality and compatibility matrix runs on supported stacks.
Imported adopter project completes adoption and governance journey checks.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized automatic serial execution, PR merge, and branch cleanup.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
